title: Epic: mvp-sync ŌĆö distributed edit + merge driver
body:
Goal: two clones can both edit bugs while offline, `jjf push` /
`jjf pull` round-trip the `bugs` bookmark via standard git
transport, and `jjf sync` resolves any conflicts automatically
without human intervention.

# Approach

The big surprise from research (`8d3e045`, archived): jj's automatic
content merge isn't enough for bug data. The moment two clones touch
nearby lines in the same bug file (title vs status, status vs a new
comment), jj's content merge produces conflicts. The natural
single-line-per-key JSON layout puts fields one line apart, well
inside the merger's "too close" threshold.

The original fix was a **file-bytes merge driver** that parsed jj's
conflict markers and ran a per-field policy. Later (in
`sync-conflict-fallback`) this was superseded by an **op-space
resolver** in `crates/jjf-storage/src/merge_ops.rs` ŌĆö the file is a
deterministic projection of the op chain, divergence resolves in
op-space, conflicts cannot surface.

Per-field policy (final, see `docs/storage-format.md` ┬¦6):

- **Scalars** (status, title, assignee, body): last-write-wins by
  `Jjf-At:` trailer timestamp.
- **Sets** (labels, dependencies): causal ŌĆö each add/remove
  tracked per key; final state is `present` iff the last write for
  that key was an add.
- **Comments**: union by `comment_id`; comments are append-only.

`jjf push` / `pull` are wrappers over `jj git push --bookmark bugs` /
`jj git fetch`, plus the op-space resolver hook on fetch divergence.

# Child tickets (all shipped during git-bug-era)

- `e2e473b` ŌĆö file-bytes merge driver (legacy, library stays in workspace).
- `07780aa` ŌĆö `sync-remote-setup`: `jjf remote add|ls|rm` wraps `jj git remote`.
- `ed7f46b` ŌĆö `sync-push-pull`: push/pull verbs end-to-end.
- `bfc732b` ŌĆö `sync-conflict-fallback`: op-space resolver replaces file-bytes path.

# Depends on

- `mvp-storage` (defines the on-disk file format the merge driver works against).

# Blocks

- `multi-client` (the daemon model assumes cross-machine sync works).

# Status

**Feature-complete** as of 2026-06-22. The full push/pull/merge loop works
end-to-end with op-space resolution (the file-as-projection model). Epic re-filed
in jjforge 2026-06-22 during the git-bug cutover (decision in archived `d12031c`).
Full status-comment history with subagent findings lives in archived git-bug ŌĆö
use `git-bug bug show 436ac0a` against `refs/bugs/*`.

Subagent flagged three non-blocking follow-ups (storage-side unit tests for
`resolve_divergence`, the now-redundant filter-on-both-files workaround at
`history.rs:62-69`, and the `OpView::touched_by_merge` flag in `read.rs` that
could be re-enabled with op-space) ŌĆö see the archived ticket for context. Surface
to a future storage-tuning ticket if they don't dissolve on their own.
